title: Chamaefistula puberula
taxonomy: Chamaefistula puberula is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Cameroon and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Chamaefistula puberula is classified under the order Fabales and the subfamily Faboideae. The plant is also known for its hairy stems and leaves, which give it its distinctive puberula characteristic. Further classification of Chamaefistula puberula may be possible at the genus and species level, but it is most commonly classified within the Fabaceae family.

intro: Chamaefistula puberula, also known as the velvet bush or puberula, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The bioactive constituents of Chamaefistula puberula include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Chamaefistula puberula include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections.
